items.mistake: Feeding raw counts into BernoulliNB without binarizing
items.fix: Use `CountVectorizer(binary=True)` or set `binarize` so features are strictly 0/1 before fitting Bernoulli NB.
items.why: Bernoulli likelihoods model presence/absence; counts greater than 1 distort the assumed Bernoulli trial semantics.
items.example: A document with "free" appearing 5 times should map to x_free=1, not 5, when using `BernoulliNB`.

items.mistake: Using MultinomialNB on negative or TF–IDF features without `fit_prior`/`force_alpha` awareness
items.fix: Keep Multinomial inputs non-negative counts (or use `ComplementNB` / linear models for TF–IDF); prefer raw counts or frequency counts for Multinomial NB.
items.why: Multinomial NB expects discrete count-like features; negative TF–IDF weights break the probabilistic interpretation.
items.example: If `TfidfVectorizer` yields negative values, switch to `CountVectorizer` + MultinomialNB or use `LinearSVC` instead.

items.mistake: Skipping smoothing (alpha=0) with sparse text data
items.fix: Set `alpha=1.0` (Laplace) or tune alpha via cross-validation; never leave zero counts for unseen word-class pairs.
items.why: A single zero θ_{y,i} zeroes the entire class posterior in log space when that word appears at test time.
items.example: A new spam token "crypto" unseen in ham training makes P(ham|x)=0 without smoothing when "crypto" appears in a test email.
